question: Another team will push invoice events to your Camunda 8.8 process over AMQP. According to the routing best-practice, what is the recommended way to land these messages on the workflow engine?

options.text: Run an AMQP consumer (or another channel: Kafka, REST, SOAP, an Apache Camel route, etc.) that receives the message and calls the Camunda API to start or correlate.
Configure the Zeebe broker with an AMQP listener so it consumes the queue directly.
Point the AMQP traffic at the Zeebe gateway port; the gateway speaks AMQP natively.
Replace the AMQP transport with HTTP — the workflow engine only accepts REST events.

a.text: The 'Technology examples' section says you need a mechanism that receives the message and routes it to the engine — citing direct API calls, AMQP or Kafka consumers, SOAP endpoints using the Camunda API, or even an Apache Camel hot-folder poller.

b.text: The Zeebe broker has no built-in AMQP listener; the docs say the workflow engine is by design not concerned with the technical part of receiving external messages.

c.text: The gateway does not speak AMQP; it offers gRPC and REST APIs that your consumer must call after picking up the AMQP message.

d.text: AMQP, JMS, Kafka and other transports are all explicitly named as valid sources — there is no requirement to replace them with HTTP.

explanation: The workflow engine is intentionally agnostic about external transports. Your job is to plug in a consumer (REST handler, AMQP or Kafka subscriber, SOAP endpoint, Apache Camel route, etc.) that receives the external event and then calls the Camunda API to create a process instance or correlate a message.

docs.title: Routing events to processes - best practices
